**Florian Ambru** (born 1928) was a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") football [defender](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") and [manager](/wiki/Manager_(association_football) "Manager (association football)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-FR-4) On 21 November 1948 he played in the first ever [CSCA București - Dinamo București](/wiki/Eternal_derby_(Romania) "Eternal derby (Romania)") derby.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Florian_Ambru&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: International career")]

Florian Ambru appeared in one friendly game at international level for [Romania](/wiki/Romania_national_football_team "Romania national football team"), under coach [Emerich Vogl](/wiki/Emerich_Vogl "Emerich Vogl") in a 6–0 victory against [Albania](/wiki/Albania_national_football_team "Albania national football team") played at the [Republic stadium](/wiki/Stadionul_Republicii "Stadionul Republicii"), [Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest").[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Florian_Ambru&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Honours")]

### Manager

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Florian_Ambru&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Manager")]

FCM Bacău

* [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"): [1955](/wiki/Divizia_B_1955 "Divizia B 1955")[[4]](#cite_note-FR-4)
